title: Default CPUShares in Inspect are 1024
body:
This is purely a display change - we weren't initializing the default value to display for the CPUShares field, which defaults to 1024. Instead, it was displaying as 0 unless the user set a CPU limit.

Fixes #4822

Change LGTM, but gating is failing with an error I've not seen before:

```
rm /go/bin/*
rm: cannot remove '/go/bin/*': No such file or directory
```
@cevich, any thoughts on the gating issue?
Oh, oops. Needs a rebase. Silly me.

> Oh, oops. Needs a rebase. Silly me.

Would it help if I added rebase instructions to our CI scripts?  Any conflict would block testing, but successful non-interactive rebasing would otherwise be transparent.  

Note: It would not actually touch the code in the PR, only rebase the runtime copy just prior to testing.

It might be a good idea just to make sure we are testing against the code
actually in the repo, not outdated code in a branch. Potential problem:
what about people who want to be testing against a specific commit for a
WIP? We probably need a flag to disable it.

> We probably need a flag to disable it?

I agree that this might cause some frustration in certain cases.  Yep, I'm thinking of just adding this before each test:

```bash
if [[ "$CI_REBASE" = "true" ]] && [[ "$CIRRUS_BRANCH" != "$DEST_BRANCH" ]]; then /run/some/rebase_script.sh; fi
```
Another idea is, support `CI_REBASE` values `true`, `false`, or `<commit ref>`.
